No Poverty.reason: The course includes a reading on 'Poverty GWR_PH,' demonstrating the application of GIS techniques to analyze poverty, which directly supports Target 1.2 on reducing poverty.
evidence: Poverty GWR_PH (Tzai-Hung Wen1, Duan-Rung Chen2, Meng-Ju Tsai3, 2010)

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit evidence in the course syllabus or readings directly relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: Core topics include 'Measures of Spatial Accessibility to Health Care' and the 'study of COVID-19,' directly addressing Target 3.8 (access to healthcare) and 3.d (health risks).
evidence: Measures of Spatial Accessibility to Health Care in a GIS Environment (Luo & Qi 2003)
Accessibility, equity and health care_PH(Tijs Neutens, 2015)

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly teaches technical GIS skills for social science, aligning with Target 4.4 (relevant skills for employment). The 'GIS Education' reading further supports this.
evidence: GIS Education in U S Public Administration Programs Preparing the Next Generation of Public Servants
